comments.body: with Requirement A: it moves the file with extension <<FileName>>.err to processed folder but the original file route also copies the file into processed folder, so there are two files created one with ext .err and other original file. For Requirement B: stopOnException=true it is runtime exception with unknown property for "stopOnException=true".
Updated on exception to stop processing, and also added sepearate block to handle exceptions, can you try it.

answers.body:
Requirement A:

Currently your onException(xxx) is logging the error but not moving or renaming the file.

You can fix this by doing the following in the onException clause:

onException(TooManyLinesException.class, NoLinesException.class)
        .handled(true)
        .log("File validation failed: ${file:name}")
        .setHeader(Exchange.FILE_NAME, simple("${file:name.noext}.err"))
        .to("file://" + PROCESSED_FOLDER)
        .stop(); // Prevent further processing of this file

Requirement B:

 onException(Exception.class)
        .handled(false)
        .log("Unhandled exception occurred. Stopping further processing: ${exception.message}");

    from("file://" + inputFolder
            + "?includeExt=dat"
            + "&eagerMaxMessagesPerPoll=true"
            + "&maxMessagesPerPoll=100"
            + "&preSort=true"
            + "&initialDelay=" + initialDelay
            + "&scheduler=spring"
            + "&scheduler.cron=" + cronExpression + "+?")
        .log("Processing file: ${file:name}")
        .bean(CreateMessageRequest.class) // May throw TooManyLinesException, NoLinesException
        .bean(SendRequestToAPI.class)     // Unhandled exceptions will stop the poll
        .to("file://" + PROCESSED_FOLDER + "?fileName=${file:name}");

This will stop processing additional files within the same poll on any exception.

title: Apache camel file component stop processing file
body:
I have a camel route as below - a) If there is an issue with reading file data the bean CreateMessageRequest it throws exception TooManyLinesException and NoLinesException but never moves to error folder. How to configure it? Or may be add file-name.err to it and move to PROCESSED_FOLDER. b) If there are exceptions in bean SendRequestToAPI while sending to API. It should immediately stop the current and the subsequent files from sending it and in next cron job should try it.

    public void configure() throws IOException {

        onException(TooManyLinesException.class, NoLinesException.class)
                .handled(true)
                .log("File LineLimitErr: ${header.CamelFileName}");

        from("file://" + inputFolder + "?includeExt=dat" + 
                "&eagerMaxMessagesPerPoll=true&maxMessagesPerPoll=100&preSort=true" +
                "&move=" + PROCESSED_FOLDER + "/${file:name}" +
                "&initialDelay=" + initialDelay + "&scheduler=spring&scheduler.cron=" + cronExpression + "+?")

                .log(LoggingLevel.INFO, "Incoming read FileName: ${headers.CamelFileName}")
                .log(LoggingLevel.INFO, "File Read: \n ${body}")
                .bean(CreateMessageRequest.class) //Error with file lines TooManyLinesException or NoLinesException
                .to(SendRequestToAPI.class) // Exceptions with sending to API

                .end();
    }
